Assignment metadata:
- objective: ## Task: M21 — Faction Reputation System

Add faction reputation tracking. Player can gain/lose reputation with factions, which affects NPC behavior, prices, and quests.

### Implementation:

**1. New DB table + Rust migration (14):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE faction_reputations (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  persona_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  faction_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  reputation INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, -- -100 to +100
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (persona_id) REFERENCES personas(id)
);
```

**2. Repository** — new `src/db/repositories/factionsRepo.ts`:
- `FactionRep` type: { id, personaId, factionName, reputation, createdAt, updatedAt }
- `listFactions(personaId)`, `updateReputation(id, delta)`, `createFaction()`

**3. AI manages factions via tags:**
- `[FACTION:+jméno:10]` — increase reputation (or starts faction at 10)
- `[FACTION:+jméno:-5]` — decrease reputation
- `[FACTION:jméno]` — shows current reputation in prompt

**4. Tag parsing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`:
- Parse `[FACTION:...]` tags
- Export `FactionMutation` type

**5. Processing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`:
- Apply faction mutations

**6. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add faction tag instructions to game tags section
- Include current faction standings when persona has any
- Instruction: "NPC reactions should reflect faction reputation. Hostile at <-50, suspicious at <-20, neutral, friendly at >20, allied at >50."

**7. UI** — add factions display to `PersonaForm.tsx`:
- Read-only list of faction standings
- Colored bar: red (-100 to -50), yellow (-49 to -20), gray (-19 to 19), green (20 to 49), gold (50 to 100)

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — add migration 14
- NEW: `src/db/repositories/factionsRepo.ts`
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — parse `[FACTION:...]` tags
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — process faction mutations
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — faction info in prompt
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — show faction list

OUTPUT: Summary, files created/modified.
- role: builder
- resolved_type: implementer

     1│ /** PromptBuilder (plan §6.2). Pure module — no DB/Tauri imports — so it can
     2│  * be unit-tested with vitest without booting the Tauri runtime. Callers
     3│  * (chatStore) load the character/persona/ledger/summary/lore data via the
     4│  * repositories and pass plain, already-resolved data in.
     5│  *
     6│  * Composes, in order:
     7│  *   1. system: character system_prompt (or default RP instructions) +
     8│  *      description + personality + scenario + mes_example + persona
     9│  *   2. `[FAKTA SVĚTA — závazná]`: active ledger facts grouped by category
    10│  *   3. activated lorebook entries (already priority-ordered by the caller)
    11│  *   4. `[DOSAVADNÍ PŘÍBĚH]`: running summary
    12│  *   5. the last `verbatimWindow` messages of history, verbatim, followed by
    13│  *      `post_history_instructions` + a `[Připomínka kánonu]` reminder of the
    14│  *      world/player facts, as a trailing system message
    15│  *
    16│  * When the estimated token total exceeds `contextBudget`, trims in this
    17│  * order until it fits (or the step is exhausted): (a0) retrieved memories
    18│  * from least relevant, (a) lore entries from
    19│  * lowest priority, (b) older verbatim messages (never below 4 most
    20│  * recent), (c) summary text from its start, (d) ledger facts
    21│  * event → quest → npc (world/player are never trimmed), (e) mes_example.
    22│  * The system core (system_prompt/description/personality/scenario/persona)
    23│  * and the most recent messages are never trimmed. Nor is the trailing
    24│  * `[Připomínka kánonu]` block — it repeats the never-trimmed world/player
    25│  * facts right before generation (where models weigh instructions most
    26│  * heavily) as a defense against genre/canon drift over long chats; it is
    27│  * only size-capped at build time, never cut under budget pressure. */
    28│ 
    29│ import type { LoreEntryLike } from "../lorebooks/activation";
    30│ import type { ConnectionConfig } from "../providers/types";
    31│ import { cosineSimilarity } from "../memory/vector";
    32│ import { estimateTokens } from "./tokenEstimate";
    33│ import { syncCountTokens } from "./tokenCounter";
    34│ 
    35│ export interface CharacterLike {
    36│   name: string;
    37│   description: string;
    38│   personality: string;
    39│   scenario: string;
    40│   systemPrompt: string;
    41│   postHistoryInstructions: string;
    42│   mesExample: string;
    43│ }
    44│ 
    45│ export interface PersonaLike {
    46│   name: string;
    47│   description: string;
    48│   gender?: string;
    49│   age?: number | null;
    50│   race?: string;
    51│   appearance?: string;
    52│   progression?: "skill" | "level" | "none";
    53│   xp?: number;
    54│   level?: number;
    55│   skills?: Array<{ name: string; level: number }>;
    56│   inventory?: Array<{ item: string; qty: number; note?: string }>;
    57│ }
    58│ 
    59│ export type LedgerCategory = "player" | "world" | "npc" | "event" | "quest";
    60│ 
    61│ export interface LedgerFactLike {
    62│   id: string;
    63│   category: LedgerCategory;
    64│   subject: string;
    65│   sub_key: string;
    66│   fact: string;
    67│   status: "active" | "archived";
    68│   locked: boolean;
    69│ }
    70│ 
    71│ export interface PromptMessage {
    72│   role: "system" | "user" | "assistant";
    73│   content: string;
    74│ }
    75│ 
    76│ export interface PromptBuilderInput {
    77│   character: CharacterLike;
    78│   persona: PersonaLike | null;
    79│   /** Ledger facts for the chat — only `status === 'active'` ones are
    80│    * rendered; archived rows are ignored (caller may pass the whole table,
    81│    * or pre-filter — either works). */
    82│   ledgerFacts: LedgerFactLike[];
    83│   summary: string | null;
    84│   /** Already activation-scanned & priority-sorted lorebook entries (see
    85│    * `lorebooks/activation.ts`). PromptBuilder only trims this list further
    86│    * when the overall budget is tight — it does not re-run the scan. */
    87│   loreEntries: LoreEntryLike[];
    88│   /** Full chat history, oldest → newest, user/assistant only (no system
    89│    * rows) — PromptBuilder takes the tail itself per `verbatimWindow`. */
    90│   history: PromptMessage[];
    91│   contextBudget: number;
    92│   /** Default 20 (`verbatim_window` setting). */
    93│   verbatimWindow?: number;
    94│   /** Optional semantic relevance per fact id (cosine similarity from
    95│    * `memory/embeddingsEngine.ts`). When present, the fact-trimming pass
    96│    * cuts the *least relevant* fact within each trimmable category instead
    97│    * of the first one found; facts missing from the map are cut first. */
    98│   factRelevance?: Record<string, number>;
    99│   /** Semantically retrieved older scenes (most relevant first) — rendered

   101│    * else under budget pressure, least relevant (last) first. */

 * Parses game tags from AI responses — inventory mutations, skill changes,
 * and level progression tags.

 * Inventory tags:
 *   [INV:+item_name]              — add item (qty 1)
 *   [INV:-item_name]              — remove item

 *   [INV:-n:item_name]            — remove n items

 *   [SKILL:name+n]                — increase skill by n levels
 *   [SKILL:name-n]                — decrease skill by n levels
 * 
 * Level tags:
 *   [LEVEL:+xp]                   — add XP (e.g. [LEVEL:+50])
 *   [LEVEL:+level]                — increase level by 1 (e.g. [LEVEL:+1])
 *
